name: Coast Guard Sector Ohio Valley
tagline: The Coast Guard on the inland rivers, covering the Ohio River system from Louisville.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Ohio Valley (Louisville, KY). CONUS full template (BAH MHA KY109 Louisville; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Inland-rivers sector: covers a huge stretch of the Ohio River and tributaries (commercial river traffic, SAR, pollution response). Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Kentucky is TRICARE East. Affordable river city. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 40299 (Louisville).

data.Role: Coast Guard inland-rivers hub for the Ohio River system
data.Mission: Commercial river traffic safety, search and rescue, and pollution response across the inland rivers
data.Note: An 'inland' Coast Guard tour, far from the ocean

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Ohio Valley, based in Louisville, covers a vast stretch of the inland river system, the Ohio River and its tributaries, keeping commercial barge traffic safe and handling search and rescue and pollution response far from any coast.
It's in Louisville, an affordable, friendly river city known for bourbon, horse racing, and a low cost of living.

data.subtitle: Louisville is an affordable river city. The main choice is which part of the metro fits your commute and schools, with Indiana suburbs just across the river.
intro: Louisville's cost of living is low, so BAH stretches well. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent or buy across the metro.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Louisville/Jefferson County and the southern Indiana suburbs across the river.

recommendations.advice: Jefferson County (Louisville) offers a wide range of neighborhoods and schools.
Southern Indiana (Jeffersonville, New Albany) is a common, affordable option a short drive over the bridge.
Louisville is affordable, so BAH tends to go far and many families buy.
Bourbon, the Derby, and parks are part of the local appeal.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in the area is limited, so most families rent or buy across the Louisville metro.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA KY109 (Louisville) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Jefferson County Public Schools (Louisville) and southern Indiana districts (Greater Clark, New Albany-Floyd) serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Sector Ohio Valley
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and Louisville-area hospitals

data.Commissary: Access to the commissary at Fort Knox (south of Louisville)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Louisville's affordable market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the metro before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Louisville market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Louisville's affordability makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on Louisville and southern Indiana before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Kentucky (or Indiana) vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Jefferson County or a southern Indiana district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; the clinic serves the assigned population.

data.a: It's a Coast Guard inland-rivers hub, based in Louisville, covering the Ohio River system: barge-traffic safety, search and rescue, and pollution response far from the ocean.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Louisville is MHA KY109.
Across the Louisville metro, including southern Indiana across the river. CG housing is limited, so most rent or buy.

data.q: Is it really a Coast Guard base?
data.a: Yes. The Coast Guard's inland-rivers mission keeps the nation's barge highways safe, far from any coast.
